findings.evidence: `rg -n "grind_pattern" code/TauCeti/Analysis/PDE/CoercivePerturbation.lean` has no hits; `code/TauCeti/Analysis/PDE/UniformEllipticEnergy.lean:201` gives a pattern for `isCoercive_energyIntegrand`.
findings.fix: Add a `grind_pattern` keyed on the base `IsCoercive`, the nonnegativity hypotheses, and `IsCoercive (energyIntegrand (A + B) b₀ (c₀ + d))`.
findings.issue: `isCoercive_energyIntegrand_add_principal_mass_of_isCoercive` is the new characteristic preservation lemma but has no `grind_pattern`, so the perturbation API will not participate in the same coercivity automation as the existing pointwise wrappers.

findings.evidence: `code/TauCeti/Analysis/PDE/CoerciveEnergy.lean:185` exposes `min_one_mass_mul_norm_sq_le_energyIntegrand_one_zero_mass_self`, while `rg -n "min_.*add_principal|principal_mass.*self" code/TauCeti/Analysis/PDE/CoercivePerturbation.lean` finds no perturbed lower-bound API.
findings.fix: Add a perturbed diagonal estimate, e.g. a `min_one_mass_mul_norm_sq_le_energyIntegrand_one_add_principal_mass_self` lemma showing the old positive constant still bounds the perturbed integrand under `hB` and `hd`.
findings.issue: `isCoercive_energyIntegrand_one_add_principal_mass` exposes only existential `IsCoercive`; the existing coercive-energy API also exposes explicit diagonal lower-bound lemmas needed before integrating pointwise estimates.

summary: The perturbation lemmas expose the core coercivity fact, but the characteristic public API is incomplete for downstream energy estimates and automation.
